narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower at the top of the order, led by Trea Turner (87.05) and Christian Yelich (81.68). Turner draws a favorable platoon advantage against Foster Griffin, compounded by high-upside speed dynamics, while Yelich benefits from strong SP-specific skill data and a prime #3 hole assignment. Junior Caminero (77.10) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (72.11) round out the high-ceiling core, both projected for top-tier production as they leverage strong platoon splits and optimal lineup positioning to drive Roto efficiency.

We are monitoring some lower-tier efficiency plays in the back end of the lineup, specifically Yordan Alvarez (61.86), whose output is hampered by a challenging L/L platoon disadvantage against Ranger Suarez. Logan O'Hoppe (46.53) and Dillon Dingler (43.70) remain our primary concerns; both are currently projected for the bottom of the order, which severely caps their scoring potential. On the positive side, Josh Smith presents an interesting contrarian play due to a substantial +16% park factor and favorable BvP history, while the "Wind Out" conditions in several matchups should provide a slight boost to our power-reliant hitters like Schwarber and PCA.
